stories.title: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before reading the prompt; OpenCode sends 7k
stories.summary: Analysis reveals Claude Code sends over 33,000 system prompt tokens before reading user input, compared to OpenCode's 7,000 — raising questions about efficiency and hidden costs in AI coding tools.

stories.title: What xAI's Grok build CLI sends to xAI: A wire-level analysis
stories.summary: A deep-dive wire-level analysis of xAI's Grok coding CLI reveals exactly what data it transmits back to xAI servers, sparking privacy and transparency concerns.

stories.title: Old and new apps, via modern coding agents
stories.summary: Mathematician Terence Tao shares his experience using modern AI coding agents to build both new and legacy applications, demonstrating practical agent capabilities.

stories.title: I love LLMs, I hate hype
stories.summary: George Hotz (geohot) writes about appreciating LLMs as genuine tools while pushing back against the surrounding hype and inflated claims in the AI industry.

stories.title: Ask HN: Add flag for AI-generated articles
stories.summary: Hacker News community debates adding a flag for AI-generated submissions as the platform struggles with increasing volume of LLM-written content.

stories.title: Migrating a production AI agent to GPT-5.6: 2.2x faster, 27% cheaper
stories.summary: Ploy.ai details their production migration to GPT-5.6, achieving 2.2x speed improvement and 27% cost reduction for their AI agent system.

stories.title: I Learned to Read Again
stories.summary: A personal essay exploring how LLMs and AI tools changed the author's reading habits and relationship with long-form text.

stories.title: Mechanistic interpretability researchers applying causality theory to LLMs
stories.summary: CACM covers new research applying causal reasoning frameworks to mechanistic interpretability, aiming to understand how LLMs actually process and reason about information.

stories.title: Flash-MSA: Accelerating Million-Token Training with Sparse Attention Kernels
stories.summary: Flash-MSA introduces sparse attention kernels that dramatically accelerate million-token training, potentially enabling much longer context windows for future LLMs.

stories.title: Z.ai GLM-5.2 Model Enters Competitive AI Race Against Anthropic and OpenAI
stories.summary: China's Z.ai launches GLM-5.2, demonstrating competitive capabilities against frontier models from Anthropic and OpenAI, signaling accelerating progress in China's AI development.
